Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Anrakugawa River (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Height: 8.5 meters (rear mound) | built = Late 4th century | burial = (Presumed) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = (Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | Excavated_items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓) | Special_notes = | map = Japan Mie | map_dot_label = Nobono Ōtsuka
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Anrakugawa River (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Anrakugawa River (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612067]] Wikidata name: Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun (能褒野王塚古墳) | Cluster = Nobono Kofun Cluster (能褒野古墳群) | image = File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun zenkei.JPG | location = {{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.884544|N|136.481969|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-24 | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳) | area = Total length: 90 meters
Height: 8.5 meters (rear mound) | built = Late 4th century | burial = (Presumed) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = (Designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | Excavated_items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓) | Special_notes = | map = Japan Mie | map_dot_label = Nobono Ōtsuka
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Keikō]]. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Anrakugawa River (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun (能褒野王塚古墳) | Cluster = Nobono Kofun Cluster (能褒野古墳群) | image = File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun zenkei.JPG | location = {{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.884544|N|136.481969|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-24 | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳) | area = Total length: 90 meters
Height: 8.5 meters (rear mound) | built = Late 4th century | burial = (Presumed) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = (Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | Excavated_items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓) | Special_notes = | map = Japan Mie | map_dot_label = Nobono Ōtsuka
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō}}. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Anrakugawa River (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun (能褒野王塚古墳) | Cluster = Nobono Kofun Cluster (能褒野古墳群) | image = File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun zenkei.JPG | location = {{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.884544|N|136.481969|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-24 | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳) | area = Total length: 90 meters
Height: 8.5 meters (rear mound) | built = Late 4th century | burial = (Presumed) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = (Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | Excavated_items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓) | Special_notes = | map = Japan Mie | map_dot_label = Nobono Ōtsuka
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō}}. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Anrakugawa River (Mie)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Height: 8.5 meters (rear mound) | built = Late 4th century | burial = (Presumed) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = (Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | Excavated_items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓) | Special_notes = | map = Japan Mie | map_dot_label = Nobono Ōtsuka
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Q11450880|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun (能褒野王塚古墳) | Cluster = Nobono Kofun Cluster (能褒野古墳群) | image = File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun zenkei.JPG | location = {{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858|1=Kameyama, Mie}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.884544|N|136.481969|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-24 | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳) | area = Total length: 90 meters
Height: 8.5 meters (rear mound) | built = Late 4th century | burial = (Presumed) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = (Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | Excavated_items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓) | Special_notes = | map = Japan Mie | map_dot_label = Nobono Ōtsuka
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Q11450880|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:能褒野王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067}} [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]

{{ill|Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun (能褒野王塚古墳) | Cluster = Nobono Kofun Cluster (能褒野古墳群) | image = File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun zenkei.JPG | location = {{ill|Kameyama|WD=Q646858|1=Kameyama, Mie}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.884544|N|136.481969|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-24 | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳) | area = Total length: 90 meters
Height: 8.5 meters (rear mound) | built = Late 4th century | burial = (Presumed) {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred_individual = (Designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} | Excavated_items = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, figurative haniwa | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓) | Special_notes = | map = Japan Mie | map_dot_label = Nobono Ōtsuka
Kofun | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-Southeast | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} }} [[File:Nobono Otsuka Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono no Haka
(dedicated to Yamato Takeru)}}]] '''Nobono Ōtsuka Kofun''' (能褒野王塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, Japan. The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja|能褒野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although the identity of the buried individual is not known, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 the tumulus as "Nobono no Haka" (能褒野墓), the resting place of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, a legendary prince and warrior of the 12th Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. It is also referred to as "Chōji-zuka" (銚子塚). This is the largest tumulus in northern Ise, measuring 90 meters in length, and is thought to have been built at the end of the 4th century (early Kofun period).The largest tumulus in Mie Prefecture is the Mihakayama Kofun (188 meters, Iga City), while the largest in Ise is the Takarazuka No.1 Kofun (pp1 meters, Matsusaka City). == Overview == [[File:Nobono-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nobono Shrine (能褒野神社)}}]] The tumulus is located on the western edge of a plateau on the northern bank of the confluence of the Mitegawa and {{ill|Anrakugawa River (Mie)|ja|安楽川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anrakugawa River (Mie)|1=Q11450880|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450880}}, tributaries of the Suzukagawa River. Surrounding the tumulus are more than ten kofun that together type the Nobono Kofun Cluster. The tumulus, previously called "Chōji-zuka" due to its resemblance to a sake bottle (銚子), was Historic Site1 as a royal burial site by the Imperial Household Ministry (now Imperial Household Agency) in 1879. Although surveys were conducted in 1929 by the Imperial Forestry Bureau and in 2013 by the Imperial Household Agency's Office of Mausolea, the main tumulus has not been excavated. The tumulus has a keyhole type, with the front section oriented southeast. Its total length is 90 meters, making it the largest tumulus in northern Ise. The structure exhibits transitional features between early and middle Kofun period designs. The rear section is constructed in two or three tiers, while the front section is built on a single tier. Excavated fragments of haniwa, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa, were found on the surface. A surrounding moat and embankment have been identified but were reconstructed in 1880, obscuring the original layout. The burial chamber is presumed to have been a vertical stone chamber, as local traditions recount that a vertical shaft large enough for several people was visible before the site's Historic Site.[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp Imperial Household Agency Mausoleum Bulletin, No. 66, 2015] == area == The tumulus area are as follows: * '''Total length''': 90 meters * '''Rear mound''': Two or three tiers ''' Diameter: Approximately 54 meters ''' Height: 8.5 meters * '''Front section''': Single tier ''' Length: 40 meters ''' Width: 40 meters ''' Height: 5.5 meters == burial goods == burial goods found at the tumulus include several haniwa fragments, particularly cylindrical and figurative haniwa. These haniwa, including those preserved at the Nobono Shrine, indicate cultural exchanges along routes from Iga (e.g., Ishiyama Kofun in Iga City) to northern Ise, leading to regions in Gifu (e.g., Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun in Ōgaki City) and Aichi (e.g., {{ill|Aotsuka Kofun|ja|青塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aotsuka Kofun|qq=}}). == Interred_individual == The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the tumulus as the burial place of Yamato Takeru, a legendary figure from the 4th century.宮内省諸陵寮編 [https://ndl.go.jp/1237123/9 "陵墓要覧"] However, archaeological evidence does not confirm this association. The tradition linking the tomb to Yamato Takeru appears to have been solidified in the 19th century. == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Nobono Shrine|ja|能褒野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|lt=Shiratori Ryo|lt=White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/White Bird Mounds (Shiratori)|qq=}} == References == {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|能褒野王塚古墳|version=99995066|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Nobono Otsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11612067|co|commons:Category:Nobono Otsuka Kofun|ja|能褒野王塚古墳}} [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
